dialogues.text: Hey. I was hoping you'd walk by. You look thin — thinner than last time. Don't make that face at me. Can you cover me for forty? I'll explain all of it, I promise, just not standing here.
dialogues.yes_response: You're so good to me. I always forget how good you are, right up until you're being good to me again.
dialogues.no_response: Oh. No — I thought we were past the part where you had to think about it. It's fine. Forget I asked.

dialogues.text: I called you yesterday. Twice, but who's counting. You didn't pick up either time. I'm not going to make it a thing. I'm just going to make it a forty.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. You always come through, even when I've been unfair about it, which is — often, lately.
dialogues.no_response: No, I get it. You've had a lot on you. I can see it sitting on you. I'll ask someone else.

dialogues.text: I've been thinking about us a lot this week. Not in a bad way — in the way you think about a song you used to know all the words to. And, also, forty.
dialogues.yes_response: See, that's the thing about you. You just get it. You never made me explain the difficult parts.
dialogues.no_response: It's fine. It can wait. Everything about us has always been very, very good at waiting.
dialogues.alt_text: You've got that look tonight. Like there's a room somewhere with someone in it, wondering where you are. I'm not going to ask whose. Forty, and I'll let you go to them.
dialogues.alt_yes_response: Thanks. You don't owe me the story. You never did, not really. Go on, then.
dialogues.alt_no_response: Right. Of course. Go — she's waiting. And I'm very good at being the thing people leave to get somewhere else.

dialogues.text: Something happened. I'm not ready to lay it all out, and I'd rather you didn't pull on the thread. I need forty, and I need you to let me keep exactly one secret today.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. I'll tell you the whole of it when I can stand to hear myself say it. I promise I will.
dialogues.no_response: Okay. I understand. I just — okay. That's a lot of okays. I'll stop now.

dialogues.text: You've been different lately. Quieter in a way that isn't about me, which is new for us. I don't know how to name it and I'm not going to try. Forty?
dialogues.yes_response: I'm here. Whatever the quiet is about. I'm not going anywhere, even when it feels like you are.
dialogues.no_response: Right. Okay. I'll drop it. I'm a champion at dropping it, honestly. Undefeated.

dialogues.text: I was going to plan something. A trip — somewhere with a coastline, somewhere neither of us has ruined with a memory yet. I need forty to hold the dates.
dialogues.yes_response: I know the timing's strange. I just want there to be a thing out ahead of us. Something to walk toward.
dialogues.no_response: Yeah. You're right. Maybe a coastline is a lot to ask of a Tuesday. Maybe some other week.

dialogues.text: I miss you. Even right now, with you close enough to touch. Is that an insane thing to say to someone's face? Forty, by the way — sorry, the two things came out tangled together.
dialogues.yes_response: I'm going to keep this. The fact that you said yes while I was being strange. I'm going to hold onto it.
dialogues.no_response: That one stings a little. But I understand. I do. I'd probably say no to me too.

dialogues.text: It's one of those days where I need you and I need forty dollars and I honestly cannot tell you which one is the bigger ask. Can you be both of those for me right now?
dialogues.yes_response: You're so good to me. I know I don't say it in a way you can keep. I'll work on that.
dialogues.no_response: It's okay. I'll call someone about the money. The other thing — I'll just carry the other thing.

dialogues.text: I trust you. Actually trust you, which I don't say lightly and have maybe never said sober. So: forty, and please don't make me explain why I need it.
dialogues.yes_response: See. That. That's exactly the reason. You let me keep my dignity even while I'm asking for things.
dialogues.no_response: I know. I know. It stings a little, but I know. You're allowed to say no to me.

dialogues.text: I love you. I don't say it enough, and when I do it's usually fastened to something I need. Like now. I need forty. Both of those are true and I refuse to put them in order.
dialogues.yes_response: I love you too. Come back soon — and I mean it as more than the thing people say. Come back.
dialogues.no_response: The first part of what I said still stands. I just wanted that on the record before you go.
